---
title: "Chapter 17 - Shraddha-Traya Vibhaga Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">श्रद्धात्रयविभागयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-16.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-18.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-17-1" data-reference="17.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-1">17.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, setting aside the rule of scripture, yet worship with faith—what is their standing, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>? <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">Sattva</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, or <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।<br>
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-2" data-reference="17.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-2">17.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The faith of the embodied is of three kinds, born of their own nature—<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>, and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>; hear of it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा ।<br>
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-3" data-reference="17.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-3">17.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The faith of each, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, accords with his nature; a person is made of faith—as his faith is, so is he.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत ।<br>
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-4" data-reference="17.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-4">17.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span> worship the gods; the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>, the yakshas and rakshasas; and the others, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>, worship the dead and the hosts of spirits.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः ।<br>
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-5" data-reference="17.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-5">17.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who practice fierce austerities not enjoined by scripture, full of hypocrisy and ego, driven by the force of desire and attachment,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः ।<br>
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-6" data-reference="17.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-6">17.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">tormenting, in their folly, the cluster of elements within the body, and me too who dwell within—know them to be of demonic resolve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः ।<br>
मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-7" data-reference="17.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-7">17.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Food, too, dear to each, is of three kinds, and so are sacrifice, austerity, and giving; hear how they differ.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः ।<br>
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श‍ृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-8" data-reference="17.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-8">17.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Foods that are juicy, smooth, firm, and pleasing to the heart are dear to the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>; they foster life, vitality, strength, health, ease, and good cheer.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः ।<br>
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-9" data-reference="17.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-9">17.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Foods that are bitter, sour, salty, very hot, sharp, harsh, and burning are craved by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>, and bring pain, grief, and sickness.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः ।<br>
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-10" data-reference="17.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-10">17.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Food that is stale, tasteless, putrid, and left overnight, leavings, and what is unfit for offering, is dear to the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् ।<br>
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-11" data-reference="17.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-11">17.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The sacrifice offered as enjoined, by those who seek no fruit, their minds settled that it is simply to be done—that is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते ।<br>
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-12" data-reference="17.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-12">17.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the sacrifice offered with an eye to its fruit, or for show, know that to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् ।<br>
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-13" data-reference="17.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-13">17.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A sacrifice offered against the rules, with no food distributed, without sacred words, gifts, or faith, they call <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् ।<br>
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-14" data-reference="17.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-14">17.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Worship of the gods, the brahmins, teachers, and the wise; purity, uprightness, celibacy, and nonviolence—these are called austerity of the body.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् ।<br>
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-15" data-reference="17.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-15">17.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Speech that gives no offense, truthful, kindly, and beneficial, and the regular recitation of scripture—this is called austerity of speech.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् ।<br>
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-16" data-reference="17.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-16">17.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of heart—this is called austerity of the mind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मनः प्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः ।<br>
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-17" data-reference="17.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-17">17.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This threefold austerity, practiced with supreme faith by the steadfast who seek no fruit, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः ।<br>
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-18" data-reference="17.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-18">17.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Austerity done for the sake of respect, honor, and reverence, or with hypocrisy, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span> here—unsteady and fleeting.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् ।<br>
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-19" data-reference="17.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-19">17.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Austerity done with deluded obstinacy, by torturing oneself, or to harm another, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः ।<br>
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-20" data-reference="17.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-20">17.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A gift given because it ought to be given, with no thought of return, in the right place and time and to a worthy person, is held to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे ।<br>
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-21" data-reference="17.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-21">17.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But a gift given for the sake of return, or with an eye to its fruit, or grudgingly, is held to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः ।<br>
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-22" data-reference="17.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-22">17.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A gift given at the wrong place and time, to the unworthy, without respect or with contempt, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते ।<br>
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-23" data-reference="17.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-23">17.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span> <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." title="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="tat">Tat</span> <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." title="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="sat">Sat</span>—this is held to be the threefold designation of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>; by it the brahmins, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, and the sacrifices were ordained of old.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ॐतत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः ।<br>
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-24" data-reference="17.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-24">17.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore the acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity prescribed by the rules are always begun with the utterance &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>&#x27; by the expounders of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः ।<br>
प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-25" data-reference="17.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-25">17.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." title="Tat: Sacred formula meaning &#x27;That&#x27;, part of the expression Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="tat">Tat</span>,&#x27; and seeking no fruit, the various acts of sacrifice, austerity, and gift are performed by those who long for liberation.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः ।<br>
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-26" data-reference="17.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-26">17.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The word &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." title="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="sat">Sat</span>&#x27; is used for what is real and for what is good; and &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." title="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="sat">Sat</span>,&#x27; O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is also applied to a praiseworthy act.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते ।<br>
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-27" data-reference="17.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-27">17.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and gift is also called &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." title="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="sat">Sat</span>,&#x27; and action for the sake of that is likewise called &#x27;<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." aria-label="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." title="Sat: Being, truth, or goodness; in 17.23 part of the sacred formula Om Tat Sat." data-glossary-id="sat">Sat</span>.&#x27;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते ।<br>
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-17-28" data-reference="17.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-17-28">17.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever is offered or given or done as austerity without faith is called &#x27;Asat,&#x27; O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>; it counts for nothing, here or hereafter.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् ।<br>
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
